title: How do you guys with full-time jobs make time for trading?
author: kisauce-666
selftext:
Being an accountant and a parent leaves me with zero time for chart watching. For the last year, I have been treating my crypto as a low effort side hustle on BYDFi and it s been surprisingly chill. I only spend about 20 mins a day usually right after the kids finally pass out.

My setup is simple. I run grid bots that just do their thing 24/7. I also do some copy trading where I follow a few traders and swap them if they start looking shaky. I only do manual trades maybe once or twice a month if the setup is super obvious.

Invested around 4200 dollars and ended up with 3000 in profit so roughly 70 percent up. The best part is the routine. I open the app check my positions scan the copy traders and then close it. If nothing s happening I just walk away.

I dont try to catch every pump anymore. I only trade what I actually understand. This platform just hit their 6th anniversary too. After seeing so many exchanges collapse having something stable is worth more than any hype to me.

At this point it is just a semi automated side income for me. No stress no losing sleep and definitely no more staring at 1 min candles until 3 am.

comments.author: Blatobrckal0
comments.body:
I would like to know as well, I'm kinda in the same boat (smaller scale and only recently started), job in finance, 2 young kids, very little time, was dabbling with crypto, some manual trades on futures, some grids but for smaller amounts. 

Curious to hear what pairs do you do grids on, what is the usual grid start and end price difference (if you let them run 24/7, you either have a very high SL or big spread between the two) and how many grids?

Thank you!

comments.author: North-Explanation219
comments.body: This feels more like optimizing time than returns.

replies.author: Artistic-East-1251
replies.body: Yeah, decent gains with minimal effort.

replies.author: Artistic-East-1251
replies.body: Yeah, decent gains with minimal effort.

comments.author: AcceptableJacket3947
comments.body:
That's awesome to hear! It's super refreshing to see someone else thriving with a low-stress, automated approach to crypto. I totally get the struggle with time – being an accountant and a parent is no joke!

I've had a really similar experience, actually. I ended up developing my own bot, and it's been working out great, pretty much exactly as you described. I actually used **Bidbits**, a company that develops trading bots, to help me build it. It's amazing how much less stressful trading becomes when you're not glued to the charts and can just let the automation do its thing. The consistent, semi-passive income without the constant anxiety is a game-changer. Congrats on the 70% up, that's seriously impressive!

comments.author: tauru_ai
comments.body:
Not kidding and promoting but that’s exactly, literally exactly why I built [Tauru.ai](https://tauru.ai) for.
It’s like having you full own personal analysis team

comments.author: North44_
comments.body: Have the same struggle. Work full time, both kids play rep sports, always on the go. I actively swing trade, keep up on macro trends and try to capitalize where possible. However, I would love to do so much more. Need something to trade between 9pm and 11pm weeknights after family down. In Ontario Canada - all leveraged trading (crypto futures etc) and prediction markets are illegal, so options are futures (ES/NQ) or develop an AI bot (which seems a bit overwhelming to learn)

comments.author: Dry_Tomorrow3632
comments.body: Most people with full-time jobs burn out because they try to trade like theyre glued to the screen 24/7, and it just doesn’t fit real life. but you basically built a system around your schedule instead of forcing your schedule around the market, which is the right way to do it.

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body:
70% up while being a parent and working full time is the actual dream. it proves that managing a system is always better than chasing every 1-minute candle.

i follow a similar low-stress routine because staring at charts all day just leads to over-trading. i spend my 20 minutes cross-referencing my setups with signalwhisper to see if the whales are actually backing the moves my bots are making. it's a quick way to get that institutional "whisper" without needing a bloomberg terminal.

stability and a consistent routine are worth more than any high-leverage hype. how do you vet your copy traders—do you focus more on their win rate or their maximum drawdown?

comments.author: tradelydev
comments.body: It only takes 10 seconds to play a warm up session and then sit around for an hour trading. On weekends make that 3hr a day. Very manageable and if you have skillz - very profitable.

comments.author: TodayProfessional444
comments.body:
This is the kind of setup I’ve been trying to get to as well low time, low stress.

Curious though do you ever feel a bit blind running it like this? Like if one of your copy traders flips bias or the grid gets caught in a trend, do you actually know *why* it’s happening or are you just reacting when things look off?
